title: Hello, Swift! 👋
description: Your very first steps in the Swift programming language.

slides.title: Meet Swift! 🦅
slides.content:
# What is Swift?

Swift is a fast, safe, and powerful programming language created by **Apple**.

It was designed to build apps for iOS, macOS, watchOS, and tvOS. It's incredibly beginner-friendly but also used by professional developers at some of the world's biggest companies!

Think of learning Swift as getting superpowers to create any app you can imagine on your iPhone.

quizData.question: Which company created the Swift programming language?
quizData.explanation: Apple created Swift in 2014 to replace their older language, Objective-C, and make app development easier and safer.

slides.title: Making Computer Talk 🗣️
slides.content:
# The print() function

How do we tell our code to say something back to us? We use the `print()` function!

When you use `print()`, you are instructing the computer to display (or *print*) text to a special window called the **console**.

```swift
print("Hello, world!")
```

The text you want to print must be wrapped inside double quotes `" "`.

quizData.question: What is the correct way to output text in Swift?
quizData.explanation: In Swift, we use the `print()` function with the text wrapped in double quotes.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Run this code and see how `print()` works! Notice how each print statement puts the text on a new line.
slides.codeSnippet:
print("Hello, Swift developer!")
print("Are you ready for an adventure?")

slides.title: Case Sensitivity 🕵️‍♂️
slides.content:
## Watch Your Capitals!

Swift is **case-sensitive**. This means `print()` works perfectly, but `Print()` with a capital 'P' will cause an error!

Always pay close attention to uppercase and lowercase letters.
